model.description: Alibaba • A balance between effectiveness, speed, and cost.

pick.rationale: Arizona edges Michigan in this extremely close Final Four matchup, primarily due to health and depth advantages. While Michigan holds a slight edge in efficiency rankings (#2 vs #3) with nearly identical adjusted efficiency margins (38.38 vs 37.56) and both teams share 31-2 records, the Wolverines have been significantly impacted by L.J. Cason's season-ending ACL injury. Head coach Dusty May has acknowledged that Michigan's bench hasn't been as good without Cason, a premier backup guard and top 3-point shooter, forcing rotation changes that have contributed to a disappointing Big Ten Tournament finish. Arizona, meanwhile, appears at full strength with star freshman Koa Peat healthy after returning from his earlier lower-body injury. In a game between two elite defensive teams (#2 and #3 nationally) with virtually identical overall profiles, Arizona's superior depth and health should prove decisive in what will be a tightly contested defensive battle.

reasoningStep.title: Arizona vs Michigan Final Four Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona selected over Michigan based on health advantage despite nearly identical efficiency metrics, as Michigan's loss of key guard L.J. Cason to ACL injury has degraded their bench production while Arizona is at full strength.
reasoningStep.evidence: Michigan ranks #2 nationally (38.38 adj_em) vs Arizona #3 (37.56 adj_em), both 31-2 records. Michigan's L.J. Cason suffered season-ending ACL tear in mid-March; coach Dusty May stated bench hasn't been as good without him. Arizona's Koa Peat returned from February injury and is healthy for Final Four. Both teams feature elite top-3 defenses.

This is very important information. Let me analyze what I've found:

1. **Michigan's L.J. Cason injury**: This is a significant blow. Cason was described as "one of the premier backup guards in college basketball" and a "top 3-point shooter."
